name: St. Louis Drive Inn
tagline: Old-guard plate lunch drive-inn near Chaminade — formica tables, walk-up counter, and food that tastes like someone's tutu made it.
mustOrder: fried chicken plate — crispy and generous
parking: Small lot on Waialae Ave; street parking available
talkStory: Operating since the 1960s near Chaminade University, St. Louis Drive Inn is old-guard plate lunch at its purest — walk-up counter, formica tables, two scoops rice, mac salad, and protein. The fried chicken is crispy perfection and the beef stew is grandma-level comfort food. While Rainbow Drive-In gets all the tourist fame, locals know St. Louis is the quieter, equally good neighborhood equivalent. Chaminade students and Kaimuki families have been eating here for generations.
